results.title: China's Latest A.I. Breakthrough Threatens America's Lead
results.content: # China’s Latest A.I. Breakthrough Threatens America’s Lead. China’s Moonshot AI unveiled a freely available artificial intelligence model that seemed to narrow the gap with cutting-edge offerings from U.S. tech companies. The contest between China and the United States for supremacy in artificial intelligence escalated on Friday when the Chinese start-up Moonshot AI released a new A.I. model that appeared to narrow the lead held by well-funded American competitors. Moonshot said that the model, Kimi K3, was the world’s largest open-source A.I. system, allowing anyone to use, modify and build on it freely. The release coincided with an address by Xi Jinping, China’s leader, in which he outlined an ambitious vision for global A.I. development that cast China as the champion of an open approach to the technology. China was “ready to work with all parties to seize the opportunities of A.I. development,” he said. If you are in Reader mode please exit and log into your Times account, or subscribe for all of The Times.

results.title: Xenco Medical Unveils XenVision, an AI-Driven Musculoskeletal Health Breakthrough at SIGGRAPH 2026, the World’s Premier Computer Graphics Conference – Ortho Spine News
results.content: # Xenco Medical Unveils XenVision, an AI-Driven Musculoskeletal Health Breakthrough at SIGGRAPH 2026, the World’s Premier Computer Graphics Conference. **Xenco Medical’s breakthrough XenVision platform, an AI-powered musculoskeletal intelligence engine.**. OrthoSpineNews –On July 19th, pioneering medical technology company Xenco Medical unveiled its breakthrough XenVision technology, an AI-powered musculoskeletal intelligence platform that combines advanced computer vision, biomechanics, and clinical AI at SIGGRAPH 2026, the world’s premier computer graphics conference organized by the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM). The launch of XenVision by Xenco Medical signifies a watershed moment in musculoskeletal care, marking the convergence of artificial intelligence, computational biomechanics, and data-driven preventive care into an unprecedented AI-powered screening platform capable of producing clinically meaningful biomechanical analyses in under two minutes. “Relentless in our pursuit of transformative frontiers in musculoskeletal care, we’ve distilled our devotion to the entire continuum of a patient’s journey into the breakthrough AI capabilities of our XenVision platform, democratizing access to high fidelity, range of motion assessments at every stage of recovery, without the need for reflective markers or wearable sensors,” said Xenco Medical Founder and CEO Jason Haider.

results.title: Nasdaq, S&P 500 drop 1% after China's latest AI ...
results.content: # Nasdaq, S&P 500 drop 1% after China’s latest AI breakthrough rattles tech stocks. Stocks in Asia and the United States fell Friday after technologicaladvances announced by a Chinese artificial intelligence company intensified concerns that the AI spending spree driving this year’s market rally could be at risk. Chinese startup Moonshot AI unveiled Kimi K3, a new open-source model that the company said closes much of the gap with models such as OpenAI’s ChatGPT and Anthropic’s Claude. The Nasdaq dropped 1.4% Friday, while the S&P 500 fell 1%. Taiwan’s benchmark stock index closed down more than 6%, while markets in Japan closed down 4% on the news. Moonshot said Kimi K3 is nearing the performance of cutting-edge models like Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5, resurfacing concerns about competition from companies in China. The S&P is down about 2% since then, while the Nasdaq is down about 6%.

results.title: China's Xi Jinping launches new AI alliance: What is it?
results.content: # China’s Xi Jinping launches new AI alliance: What is it? *Beijing is likely to use the WAICO alliance to influence global AI regulations, analysts say.*. Chinese President Xi Jinping poses for a group photo with other attendees before the opening ceremony for the World AI Conference in Shanghai, China, July 17, 2026 [Ng Han Guan/Reuters]. Chinese President Xi Jinping on Friday urged countries to cooperate on artificial intelligence and ensure no country dominates the technology – in an apparent jab at the United States. As Chinese AI models gain ground globally, Beijing appears to be casting itself as a leader of the new global AI order, challenging the US’s influence in the development and regulation of the fast-evolving technology. A humanoid robot that cooks is displayed at the Xi Xiang booth during the World Artificial Intelligence Conference (WAIC) in Shanghai, China, July 17, 2026. A humanoid robot that cooks is displayed at the Xi Xiang booth during the World Artificial Intelligence Conference (WAIC) in Shanghai, China, July 17, 2026 [Go Nakamura/Reuters].

results.title: Department of Energy's New AI-for-Science ‘Genesis Mission’ Awards Funding to 5 UT Research Projects
results.content: # Department of Energy’s New AI-for-Science ‘Genesis Mission’ Awards Funding to 5 UT Research Projects. Five UT teams have been awarded funding from the DOE’s Genesis Mission, an initiative working to harness artificial intelligence for advances in energy dominance, discovery science and national security. Five University of Texas at Austin teams have been awarded funding from the U.S. Department of Energy’s Genesis Mission, an initiative working to harness artificial intelligence for advances in energy dominance, discovery science and national security. UT will lead two of the projects and serve as a partner institution on three others. According to the Department of Energy, the Genesis Mission is building the world’s most powerful integrated science discovery platform. **Kevin Clarno**, an associate professor in the Walker Department of Mechanical Engineering and affiliated faculty with the Oden Institute, will lead a team of eight UT researchers as part of the Prometheus project, a $60 million effort led by the Idaho National Laboratory to reduce the time and cost of developing nuclear energy.

results.title: UT Secures $20M NSF Grant To Pioneer Breakthroughs in Automated Materials Discovery
results.content: The University of Tennessee, Knoxville, has received $20 million from the National Science Foundation to establish ATHENA (Advanced Testbed for High-throughput Experimentation in Nano- and Atomic Science), one of 20 research hubs in the U.S. that will form a national network of AI-powered laboratories to accelerate scientific discovery. Sergei Kalinin, Weston Fulton Professor in the Tickle College of Engineering at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. Led by Sergei Kalinin, Weston Fulton Professor in UT’s Tickle College of Engineering; Mashid Ahmadi, associate professor of materials science and engineering; and Hairong Qi, Gonzalez Family Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, ATHENA brings together a multidisciplinary team of 12 UT faculty members along with key collaborators at Northwestern University and Johns Hopkins University. The ATHENA initiative aims to revolutionize materials discovery by dramatically accelerating the design, synthesis, characterization and autonomous optimization of advanced materials, enabling the rapid identification of materials with the greatest potential for real-world applications.

results.title: National artificial intelligence competency framework for teachers launched in Egypt
results.content: # National artificial intelligence competency framework for teachers launched in Egypt. UNESCO and the Ministry of Education and Technical Education of Egypt officially launched the national "Artificial intelligence competency framework for teachers" on 3 June 2026. The launch positions Egypt among the first countries worldwide to develop and adopt a nationally contextualized AI competency framework for teachers, based on UNESCO’s global framework and tailored to Egypt’s educational priorities, needs and aspirations. AI Framework for teachers Egypt. H.E. Dr Mohamed Abdellatif, Minister of Education and Technical Education of Egypt (right), and Mr Robert Parua, Officer-in-Charge of the UNESCO Cairo Office present Egypt's national AI Competency Framework for Teachers. Building on the Ministry’s ongoing efforts to integrate technology, coding and digital learning into education, the framework aims to equip teachers with the knowledge, skills and ethical understanding necessary to use AI responsibly and effectively in teaching and learning.

results.title: Ohio State researchers to lead national teams exploring science, AI innovation
results.content: The Genesis Mission will harness AI for breakthroughs in energy dominance, discovery science and national security. | photo:The Ohio  State University | The Ohio State University |. The Ohio State University continues to build momentum as a national leader in artificial intelligence research and innovation. This week, two teams led by Ohio State researchers joined a $293 million initiative sponsored by the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) to harness AI for breakthroughs in energy dominance, discovery science and national security. The Genesis Mission is designed to double America’s scientific productivity through DOE’s world-class scientific capabilities, advanced AI, high-performance computing and the nation’s leading researchers. The goal of the mission is to transform how scientific discovery is conducted and continue to grow the nation’s leadership in technology and discovery. “We are pleased that researchers from two Ohio State-led teams have been selected through the U.S. Department of Energy’s Genesis Mission, a groundbreaking initiative that seeks to accelerate scientific discovery through the use of artificial intelligence,” said John M.

results.title: Breakthroughs in Chinese AI models threaten U.S. lead in tech race
results.content: #### Get news alerts from PBS News. # Breakthroughs in Chinese AI models threaten U.S. lead in tech race. Breakthroughs in Chinese AI models are sending shockwaves through Washington and Silicon Valley. Chinese companies are narrowing the AI gap despite vast differences in computing power and resources. It comes as the White House is planning to redirect billions in funding from universities to individuals in a bid to accelerate AI innovation. Geoff Bennett discussed more with Amrith Ramkumar. ## Listen to this Segment. ## More From This Episode.
